needle_load.note: Claude has a 3s idle before needle_load; combined idle+needle_load Claude=8s vs GPT-4o=2.5s (delta 5.5s).

suture_cut.note: Same boundary disagreement as V11/V14 — Claude assigns 33s to cut, GPT-4o only 4.5s and rolls the late-task time into the throws. Sum of throws+cut: Claude 82s vs GPT-4o 84.5s (delta 2.5s) — essentially agreement.

gap_visible.note: First V-series monitor-filmed video with NO drain-gap divergence. Claude's closure call is 'appears_closed' (very_low conf) vs GPT-4o 'complete' — both effectively agree there is no gap.

placement_alignment.note: Both teachers low/very_low confidence; estimates within 0.5 mm.

fls_score_reconciliation.reconciliation_note: Strong agreement. Completion time essentially identical (Δ1.0s). The 2.5pt penalty gap is a 0.5pt placement + 2.0pt unexplained Claude penalty margin (Claude applied 5.5 total penalties but only 3.5 were placement — the extra 2.0 may be an implicit time-buffer penalty from the drain-quality uncertainty). GPT-4o's 483.0 is the cleaner point estimate; midpoint 481.25 is a reasonable reconciled value.

systematic_findings.boundary_disagreement_confirmed: V12 confirms the V11/V14 pattern — Claude and GPT-4o disagree on third_throw vs suture_cut boundary but agree on their sum. V12 delta on sum is only 2.5s (tightest so far). Continue to recommend training on total_time + penalties, not raw phase durations.
systematic_findings.drain_gap_agreement_breakthrough: V12 is the FIRST V-series comparison where Claude and GPT-4o agree on the drain closure. Claude's 'appears_closed' with very_low confidence matches GPT-4o's 'complete'. Counter-evidence to the Claude monitor-bias finding from V11/V14 — the bias is not universal, only triggers on specific video quality/angle combinations.
